pokemon:trubbish.name: Trubbish
pokemon:trubbish.summary: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.
Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.
Wanting more garbage, they follow people who litter. They always belch poison gas.
It gorges on trash until its stomach is full. Then it belches toxic gas. An unlucky whiff of gas will put a person in the hospital.
The combination of garbage bags and industrial waste caused the chemical reaction that created this Pokémon.
This Pokémon was born from a bag stuffed with trash. Galarian Weezing relish the fumes belched by Trubbish.
Unsanitary places are what they like best. They can be spotted in Alola, often with Grimer in hot pursuit.
Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.
If a young Pokémon or child breathes in the toxic gas that Trubbish belches out, it could be a life-threatening situation.
Poisonous gas leaks out of it when it breathes. Muk that catch a whiff of that stench will come drooling.
The combination of garbage bags and industrial waste caused the chemical reaction that created this Pokémon.
Wanting more garbage, they follow people who litter. They always belch poison gas.
The combination of garbage bags and industrial waste caused the chemical reaction that created this Pokémon.
Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

pokemon:garbodor.name: Garbodor
pokemon:garbodor.summary: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.
It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.
Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.
For a time, their numbers increased explosively in Alola. Since the arrival of Grimer, their population has decreased dramatically.
It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.
The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.
Beware the poisonous liquid it shoots from its right arm. If even a little of it gets on you, you’ll experience the effects of the unidentified toxin.
This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body. The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.
Some say the reason Garbodor in Alola are a little stronger than their counterparts elsewhere is the presence of Muk, their natural enemy.
It locks opponents in place with its left hand, immobilizing them by entirely dousing their bodies with poisonous liquid.
They absorb garbage and make it part of their bodies. They shoot a poisonous liquid from their right-hand fingertips.
Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.
It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.
Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.
